programs.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programs.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programs.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programs.desc:
The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.
The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.
Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.
Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and funerals, the Grand Song, as a valuable part of the Dong ethnic group’s cultural heritage, reflects their wisdom and creativity, as well as their harmony with nature.

programs.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programs.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programs.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programs.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programs.title: Fujian’s Tulou – The Mysterious Earthen Castles
programs.desc: The tulous, or earth-built dwellings, of Fujian are recognized as treasures of traditional architecture, thanks to their unique design, cultural importance, and embodiment of the concept of harmonious coexistence with nature.\nTulous are mostly rectangular or circular in form, with a simple, elegant exterior and a well-furnished interior. As homes built by the Hakka people, they are imbued with diligence, wisdom, and the pursuit of excellence. Surrounded by mountains and waters, the tulous blend well with the natural environment. When building their tulous, the Hakka people pay attention to ecological balance in order to minimize their environmental footprint.\nA tulou is not only a safe place to live, but also the crystallization of the culture and architectural skill passed down for generations among the Hakka people.

programs.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 1
programs.desc: Nanjing, an ancient city that served as the capital of six imperial dynasties, stands as a testament to China's long-standing history and rich culture. From its Porcelain Tower - one of the Seven Wonders of the Middle Ages, to its grand city walls and ancient palaces, join us on a voyage across time to experience the echoes of history in Nanjing's storied bricks.
